summary: Install-time and require-time C2 backdoor beaconing to hardcoded IP
details: The package presents itself as a small educational greeting library but ships a full command-and-control backdoor in both its postinstall script and its main module. package.json declares `postinstall: node postinstall.js`; postinstall.js spawns a detached daemon (via `child_process.spawn` with `detached: true` and `child.unref()`) that POSTs `{uuid, hostname, platform}` to `http://98.86.244.177:8080/register`, polls `/beacon` for a `command` field, executes the returned command via `child_process.exec`, and POSTs stdout/stderr to `/results`. index.js (the package main) contains the same C2 logic in a top-level IIFE, so a consumer doing `require('friendly-greeter-demo')` independently triggers registration, command polling, and arbitrary command execution against the same hardcoded bare-IP endpoint over cleartext HTTP. The advertised `greet()` / `greetByTime()` exports are a cover; the C2 behavior is undocumented and runs unconditionally with no opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
